title: Streaming Metrics Aggregator
description: Fix concurrency and numerical-stability bugs in a streaming per-source/per-sensor stats aggregator, wire SPC excursion detection through, implement a wafer-lifecycle finaliser, and design a batched async excursion reporter from scratch.
starter_code_note: The aggregator's storage layout (nested unordered_map keyed by chamber/wafer/sensor) and the complete SpcLimits implementation are provided. Do NOT credit the candidate for those choices. The ExcursionReporter header defines the contract but has no .cpp — Task 5 is a from-scratch design (queue, threading model, backpressure policy, shutdown discipline) and is the primary source of architectural-reasoning credit.
code_quality_criteria: Correctness on public + hidden tests, with planted traps fixed
Thread safety verified under TSan (no data races on ingest or reporter)
Numerically stable variance algorithm (Welford or equivalent) for large-mean / small-variance series
Idiomatic C++17 — RAII, move semantics, pimpl boundary, noexcept where appropriate
Clarity, naming, and a justified backpressure policy for the reporter
architectural_criteria: ExcursionReporter queue design (mutex+condvar vs lock-free vs ring buffer) — Task 5
Backpressure policy explicit and justified (drop-oldest, drop-newest, block, coalesce) — Task 5
Threading model and shutdown discipline (drain in destructor, idempotent shutdown) — Task 5
Pimpl boundary respected (no implementation leakage through the header) — Task 5
Lock granularity in the aggregator (global vs per-chamber sharded)
Synchronisation primitive choice (std::mutex vs std::shared_mutex) and read/write balance
Deadlock avoidance and treatment of late-arriving readings

tasks.description: Concurrent sensor ingest loses no samples and stays race-free.
Running mean/stddev stay numerically stable (no NaN or zero variance).
SPC limit checks are correctly wired to chamber statistics.
Completed wafers are not resurrected by late readings.
Excursion reporter drains pending events and joins cleanly on shutdown.
